# Mediflow Hyper\-Local Healthcare Ecosystem

## Standard Operating Procedure \(SOP\) — Operational & Technical Blueprint

__Document Control ID:__ MF\-SOP\-2026\-V1

__Effective Date:__ May 22, 2026

__Status:__ Approved / Active

__Author:__ Mediflow Systems Integration & Architecture

__Target Audience:__ Clinic Managers, Compounders, Doctors, Pharmacists, Lab Managers, and System Support Engineers\.

## 1\. Document Overview & Purpose

### 1\.1 Purpose

This Standard Operating Procedure \(SOP\) establishes a uniform, friction\-free workflow across all interconnected healthcare nodes in a Mediflow hyper\-local B2B healthcare "Pod" \(comprising a Clinic, a Partner Laboratory, and a Partner Pharmacy\)\.

### 1\.2 Scope

This document covers the entire lifecycle of a patient encounter, beginning with clinic check\-in, progressing through digital prescription and test requisition routing, physical sample collection, automated reagent and drug stock allocation, unified UPI split\-billing, and concluding with automated WhatsApp\-based clinical follow\-ups and ledger settlements\.

## 2\. Core Stakeholders & Role Definers

| __Stakeholder Role__ | __Primary Interface__ | __Operational Mandate__ | __System Restrictions \(RBAC\)__ |

| __Doctor / Clinician__ | Clinic Dashboard | Conduct consultations, select drugs, order LOINC tests, review historical AI\-analyzed reports\. | Full access to clinical encounter history; restricted from pharmacy purchase margins\. |

| __Compounder / Receptionist__ | Mobile\-optimized Portal | Scan physical prescription notes, register new patient demographics, initiate the WhatsApp patient bot, handle appointments\. | Write\-only access to new patients; no access to historical clinical notes or diagnostic results\. |

| __Lab Technician / Manager__ | Lab Dashboard | Receive diagnostic requisitions, draw blood/fluid samples, run analyzers, input data, track reagent consumption\. | Access to specific lab orders; strictly blocked from reviewing doctor's internal clinic notes\. |

| __Pharmacist / POS Operator__ | LitePOS Counter Interface | Fulfill incoming automated medicine reservations, process payments, dispatch orders, maintain FEFO inventory rules\. | Read\-only access to prescribed drugs, dosages, and patient names; strictly blocked from diagnostic data\. |

| __The Patient \(End\-User\)__ | WhatsApp Chatbot Agent | Review appointment reminders, download PDF lab report cards, receive comparative analysis alerts, make unified UPI payments\. | Access restricted to their own verified medical and billing history via OTP login\. |

## 3\. Step\-by\-Step Operating Procedures \(The "Happy Path" Care Loop\)

\[Phase 1: Check\-In\] ────> \[Phase 2: Consult & e\-Rx\] ────> \[Phase 3: Lab Requisition\]  
                                                                  │  
\[Phase 6: Follow\-Up\] <──── \[Phase 5: Split Billing\] <──── \[Phase 4: Pharmacy Hold\]  
  


### Phase 1: Clinic Check\-In & Care Initiation \(Compounder Workflow\)

1. __Patient Arrival:__ The patient presents at the clinic desk\.
2. __Identification Check:__ The Compounder queries the Mediflow database using the patient's mobile number or Ayushman Bharat Health Account ID \(ABHA ID\)\.
	- *If New Patient:* The Compounder registers the patient's name, phone, age, biological sex, allergies, and existing chronic conditions directly into the patient\_registry database\.
3. __Scanning & Bot Activation:__ The Compounder clicks __"Initiate Mediflow Loop"__ on their mobile portal:
	- This automatically creates a session in whatsapp\_sessions setting the state to AWAITING\_WELCOME\.
	- The WhatsApp Bot pushes an automated welcome payload to the patient's WhatsApp containing a secure dynamic OTP authorization link to collect the patient's time\-bound digital consent\.
4. __Consent Logging:__ The patient taps "Grant Access" inside their WhatsApp chat\. The system commits an opt\-in flag to the patient\_consents table\.

### Phase 2: Consultation & Clinical Decision Support \(Doctor Workflow\)

1. __Patient Call:__ The Doctor opens the active encounters dashboard\.
2. __Reviewing Past Records:__ If the patient has historical lab reports on file, the Mediflow LLM comparative engine automatically surfaces a timeline analysis on the Doctor's screen showing biomarker variations \(e\.g\., Blood Sugar trajectory or Kidney Creatinine trends\) over time\.
3. __E\-Prescription Creation:__
	- The Doctor documents clinical notes and inputs medications from the global drug master index\.
	- The Doctor checks the standard boxes for diagnostic panels \(linked to standard LOINC\-coded test definitions in the master\_test\_catalog\)\.
4. __Finalization:__ The Doctor clicks __"Submit Encounter & Route"__\.
	- __System Automation Trigger:__ The backend instantly executes two webhooks:
		- __Action A:__ Pushes the drug list to the partner pharmacy, creating a temporary inventory\_holds lock on matching drugs using First\-Expiry\-First\-Out \(FEFO\) rules\.
		- __Action B:__ Pushes the diagnostic tests directly to the partner laboratory's sample collection queue\.

### Phase 3: Laboratory Sample Processing & Reagent Deduction \(Lab Workflow\)

1. __Sample Collection:__ The patient arrives at the diagnostic lab in the building\. The Lab Technician selects the patient’s active profile from their Mediflow Lab Dashboard\.
2. __Barcode Labeling:__ The lab printer outputs barcode stickers mapping to the specific lab\_samples row generated by the doctor's requisition\. The tech attaches labels to the blood/urine collection tubes\.
3. __Analytical Processing:__ The tech processes the sample inside the clinical analyzer\.
4. __Result Submission:__ The technician enters the quantitative results into the dashboard structured forms\.
5. __Reagent Deduction Logic:__ Upon clicking "Publish and Verify Report":
	- The database function on\_lab\_test\_completed executes\.
	- The system references reagent\_requirements and deducts the exact volume of chemical reagents \(e\.g\., ![](data:image/png;base64,) of analyzer wash solution, ![](data:image/png;base64,)\) from the laboratory's inventory ledger\.
6. __Report Delivery:__ The report is converted to a structured JSON payload and a secure, signed PDF URL is uploaded\. The doctor receives an instant real\-time ping, and the patient receives a direct WhatsApp report card link\.

### Phase 4: Pharmacy Dispensing & Inventory Management \(Pharmacy Workflow\)

1. __Fulfillment Queue:__ The pharmacist views the inventory\_holds panel on the LitePOS screen\.
2. __Verification & Packing:__ The medicine basket is already prepared based on the automated hold\. The pharmacist scans the medicine barcodes to confirm the drug names, batch numbers, and expiry thresholds align with the doctor's digital prescription\.
3. __Stock Update:__ Once confirmed, the system transitions the inventory status from held to dispensed, adjusting the store's physical inventory count in real\-time\.

### Phase 5: Unified Billing & Financial Settlement \(Fintech Layer\)

1. __Invoice Generation:__ The platform generates a single unified\_invoices record aggregating:
	- __Doctor Consultation Fee:__ ![](data:image/png;base64,)
	- __Diagnostic Lab Fees:__ ![](data:image/png;base64,)
	- __Pharmacy Retail Fees:__ ![](data:image/png;base64,)
2. __Payment Display:__ The pharmacist or lab technician displays a single, dynamically generated UPI QR code on their screen representing the total sum:![](data:image/png;base64,)
3. __Split Routing \(The Settlement Ledger\):__ The patient scans the dynamic UPI QR code\. The moment the payment gateway callback returns a success response:
	- __The Platform Fee Cut:__ The system automatically deducts ![](data:image/png;base64,) from each sub\-amount as the Mediflow SaaS processing fee\.
	- __Direct Split Distribution:__
		- The net clinic consultation fee ![](data:image/png;base64,) is routed directly to the Clinic's nodal merchant bank account\.
		- The net pharmacy collection ![](data:image/png;base64,) is routed to the Pharmacy's merchant account\.
		- The diagnostic fee ![](data:image/png;base64,) is split: the net lab earnings are routed to the laboratory account, while any pre\-configured doctor referral commission \(e\.g\., ![](data:image/png;base64,), governed by entity\_partnerships\) is automatically transferred into the Doctor’s settlement ledger\.

### Phase 6: Automated WhatsApp Follow\-Up & Retention Loops

1. __Post\-Encounter Follow\-Up:__ 7 days post\-encounter, the WhatsApp engine schedules and sends a personalized message to the patient\.
2. __Medication Refills:__ If the prescription contained a chronic care medication \(e\.g\., Metformin for 30 days\), the patient receives a prompt on Day 25: "Your diabetes medication is running low\. Tap![](data:image/png;base64,)  
to reserve a fresh pack at Ground Floor Pharmacy\."
3. __Virtual Follow\-Up Booking:__ For acute conditions, the system offers a virtual appointment link at a pre\-configured promotional discount rate \(e\.g\., ![](data:image/png;base64,) off consultation fee\)\.
4. __Automated Scheduling:__ If the patient books, the new appointment is logged in clinic\_staff appointments, and the care loop repeats\.

## 4\. Exception Handling & Troubleshooting Guidelines

### 4\.1 Pharmacy Out\-of\-Stock \(OOS\) Rollback Protocol

- __Issue:__ A drug prescribed by the doctor is out\-of\-stock at the partner pharmacy during hold validation\.
- __Protocol:__
	1. The Mediflow system immediately releases any incomplete inventory\_holds for that medication\.
	2. The system triggers a real\-time notification to the Doctor's clinic dashboard: *"Warning: Amoxicillin is OOS at partner pharmacy\. Click to route to alternative catalog or send paper fallback prescription\."*
	3. The patient is notified via WhatsApp that their medication is being arranged or can be fulfilled via an alternative partner shop\.

### 4\.2 Consent Revocation \(DPDPA 2023 Enforcement\)

- __Issue:__ The patient decides to withdraw clinical data sharing permission between the doctor, lab, and pharmacy\.
- __Protocol:__
	1. The patient types "STOP CONSENT" or toggles off the data access option inside their WhatsApp menu\.
	2. The database updates patient\_consents and sets revoked\_at = now\(\)\.
	3. All Supabase Row\-Level Security \(RLS\) queries to encounters or lab\_results will immediately fail for the pharmacy and lab accounts when querying this patient's ID, protecting the patient's privacy and ensuring full compliance with the law\.

## 5\. Security, Access Controls \(RBAC\), and Compliance Checklists

### 5\.1 Weekly Security Checklists \(To be executed by the Pod Admin\)

- ![](data:image/png;base64,)Verify that all clinic staff accounts are active and linked to correct profiles records in Supabase\.
- ![](data:image/png;base64,)Audit the activity\_logs table to ensure no unauthorized pharmacy staff have tried to query clinical records or patient notes\.
- ![](data:image/png;base64,)Check that Razorpay/Cashfree API connection endpoints are reporting ![](data:image/png;base64,) operational uptime\.
- ![](data:image/png;base64,)Inspect the whatsapp\_sessions database for any anomalous bot hangs or deadlocks in state changes\.
